name: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland — Karaoke Reader (Demo)
description: A karaoke-style ALL-CAPS narrated reading of Alice in Wonderland (Ch. I). Each passage = one performance clip: illustration + voice-over + per-line highlighted text. Lewis Carroll, 1865 — public domain. Excerpts from Chapter I, 'Down the Rabbit-Hole'.

sections.Text: ALICE WAS BORED BY THE RIVER. SUDDENLY A WHITE RABBIT RAN BY. IT HAD PINK EYES. “OH DEAR! OH DEAR!” IT SAID. “I SHALL BE LATE!” IT TOOK A WATCH FROM ITS WAISTCOAT. ALICE JUMPED UP AND RAN AFTER IT.
sections.Narration: Alice was bored by the river. Suddenly a white rabbit ran by. It had pink eyes. “oh dear! oh dear!” it said. “i shall be late!” It took a watch from its waistcoat. Alice jumped up and ran after it.
sections.Illustration prompt: Classic children's-book watercolor: a white rabbit standing upright wearing a little checked waistcoat, holding a round gold pocket watch and looking worried; a curious young girl in a blue dress watching from behind a tree on a sunny riverbank; warm, gentle, John Tenniel-inspired but soft and colorful.

background_audio.note: low gentle music — set youtube_video_id/volume when chosen

overlays.content: ALICE WAS BORED BY THE RIVER.
SUDDENLY A WHITE RABBIT RAN BY.
IT TOOK A WATCH FROM ITS WAISTCOAT.
ALICE JUMPED UP AND RAN AFTER IT.

sections.Text: DOWN WENT ALICE AFTER IT. THE RABBIT-HOLE WENT STRAIGHT DOWN. THEN IT DIPPED SUDDENLY DOWN. DOWN, DOWN, DOWN SHE FELL. WOULD THE FALL NEVER END? SHE FELL PAST CUPBOARDS AND SHELVES. SHE FELL VERY, VERY SLOWLY.
sections.Narration: Down went alice after it. The rabbit-hole went straight down. Then it dipped suddenly down. Down, down, down she fell. Would the fall never end? She fell past cupboards and shelves. She fell very, very slowly.
sections.Illustration prompt: Storybook illustration: a young girl in a blue dress falling gently down a very deep round well, arms out, dress billowing; the curved walls are lined with little cupboards, bookshelves, jars and a map; warm lamplight, dreamy, slightly tumbling perspective.

background_audio.note: low gentle music — set youtube_video_id/volume when chosen

overlays.content: SHE FELL PAST CUPBOARDS AND SHELVES.

sections.Text: ALICE CAME TO A LONG HALL. ALL ROUND WERE LOCKED DOORS. THEN SHE FOUND A TINY DOOR. IT WAS FIFTEEN INCHES HIGH. A LITTLE GOLDEN KEY OPENED IT. BEHIND IT WAS A LOVELY GARDEN. BUT ALICE WAS FAR TOO BIG. SHE COULD NOT EVEN FIT HER HEAD!
sections.Narration: Alice came to a long hall. All round were locked doors. Then she found a tiny door. It was fifteen inches high. A little golden key opened it. Behind it was a lovely garden. But alice was far too big. She could not even fit her head!
sections.Illustration prompt: Children's-book watercolor: a GIANT young girl in a blue dress kneeling and bending all the way down to peer through a TINY door only about fifteen inches high set in the base of a wall; the little door is unmistakably MUCH smaller than her head; through the small open doorway we glimpse a bright sunny garden of roses, fountains and hedges; a little golden key on the floor; warm, classic, whimsical. The door must look tiny and the girl must look huge.

background_audio.note: low gentle music — set youtube_video_id/volume when chosen

overlays.content: ALICE CAME TO A LONG HALL.
THEN SHE FOUND A TINY DOOR.
A LITTLE GOLDEN KEY OPENED IT.
BEHIND IT WAS A LOVELY GARDEN.
BUT ALICE WAS FAR TOO BIG.
SHE COULD NOT EVEN FIT HER HEAD!

sections.Text: ON THE TABLE WAS A BOTTLE. A LABEL SAID: “DRINK ME.” ALICE TOOK A LITTLE SIP. IT TASTED LIKE CHERRY TART. “WHAT A CURIOUS FEELING!” SHE SAID. SHE WAS SHUTTING UP LIKE A TELESCOPE. NOW SHE WAS TEN INCHES HIGH!
sections.Narration: On the table was a bottle. A label said: “drink me.” Alice took a little sip. It tasted like cherry tart. “what a curious feeling!” she said. She was shutting up like a telescope. Now she was ten inches high!
sections.Illustration prompt: Storybook watercolor: a little glass bottle with a paper label reading 'DRINK ME' tied with string, sitting on a three-legged glass table; a young girl in a blue dress shrinking small beside it, looking surprised; soft magical light.

background_audio.note: low gentle music — set youtube_video_id/volume when chosen

overlays.content: ON THE TABLE WAS A BOTTLE.
SHE WAS SHUTTING UP LIKE A TELESCOPE.
NOW SHE WAS TEN INCHES HIGH!

sections.Text: UNDER THE TABLE WAS A CAKE. ON IT, CURRANTS SPELLED: “EAT ME.” “I’LL EAT IT,” SAID ALICE. SHE TOOK ONE LITTLE BITE. “WHICH WAY? WHICH WAY?” SHE ASKED. THEN UP, UP, UP SHE GREW. NOW SHE WAS NINE FEET TALL!
sections.Narration: Under the table was a cake. On it, currants spelled: “eat me.” “i’ll eat it,” said alice. She took one little bite. “which way? which way?” she asked. Then up, up, up she grew. Now she was nine feet tall!
sections.Illustration prompt: Storybook watercolor: a small round frosted cake on the floor with the words 'EAT ME' spelled in dark currants on top; a young girl in a blue dress suddenly grown very tall, her head near the ceiling, looking astonished; warm whimsical light.

background_audio.note: low gentle music — set youtube_video_id/volume when chosen

overlays.content: UNDER THE TABLE WAS A CAKE.
THEN UP, UP, UP SHE GREW.
NOW SHE WAS NINE FEET TALL!

sections.Text: “CURIOUSER AND CURIOUSER!” CRIED ALICE. SHE WAS SO SURPRISED. SHE FORGOT TO TALK PROPERLY. “NOW I’M OPENING OUT LIKE A TELESCOPE!” “GOODBYE, FEET!” SHE CALLED. HER FEET WERE SO FAR AWAY NOW. OH, WHAT A STRANGE DAY!
sections.Narration: “curiouser and curiouser!” cried alice. She was so surprised. She forgot to talk properly. “now i’m opening out like a telescope!” “goodbye, feet!” she called. Her feet were so far away now. Oh, what a strange day!
sections.Illustration prompt: Whimsical storybook watercolor: a young girl in a blue dress grown impossibly tall, looking down and waving cheerfully at her own tiny feet far below; playful, warm, gentle humor, classic children's-book style.

background_audio.note: low gentle music — set youtube_video_id/volume when chosen

overlays.content: “NOW I’M OPENING OUT LIKE A TELESCOPE!”
HER FEET WERE SO FAR AWAY NOW.
